extra.reasoning: The output `acuity` is computed solely from abdominal_pain, leg_swelling, and rash. The input `dyspnea` does not appear in any rule (r1 keys on abdominal_pain, r2 on leg_swelling, r3 on rash) nor in the default (routine). Because acuity is entirely independent of dyspnea, for any fixed assignment of the other three inputs the acuity value with dyspnea=false equals the acuity value with dyspnea=true. Setting dyspnea true therefore leaves acuity unchanged, which trivially satisfies "no lower than before." Concretely: abdominal_pain=true yields emergent via r1 (priority 3) regardless of dyspnea; abdominal_pain=false with leg_swelling or rash true yields urgent via r2/r3 regardless of dyspnea; otherwise the default routine applies regardless of dyspnea. In all reachable input combinations, toggling dyspnea on does not change (and hence never lowers) acuity. The monotonicity property holds over the entire input space.
